title: Merge feat/tui-position-management branch to main
description: The TUI position management branch contains several sessions of work (import, close, reconcile from dashboard, escape-safe input, arrow shortcuts, startup sync refactor, side normalizer, realtime signal runner) and the test suite is green. Run parallel_rspec, pass StandardRB and Brakeman, then open PR and merge.

title: Add kill switch / trading halt mechanism
description: No global kill switch exists. Monitoring jobs (margin, basis, expiry) log warnings but cannot halt new order placement. Need a lightweight on/off gate (e.g. a Rails.cache flag, a DB column on a Settings model, or an ENV override) that FuturesExecutor and trading services check before placing any order. Should be settable via rake task and/or CLI command.

title: Wire GenerateSignalsJob to FuturesExecutor for real order placement
description: GenerateSignalsJob evaluates strategy signals but never executes them — it only logs. The executor path is stubbed with a TODO comment. Need to call FuturesExecutor#consider_entry (or a new place_order method) when a signal fires, with appropriate guards (paper-trading mode flag, kill switch, position limits).

title: Add end-to-end integration spec for signal → executor flow
description: There are unit specs for strategies, signal alerts, and trading services individually, but no spec that exercises the full path: strategy emits signal → RealTimeSignalEvaluator persists it → GenerateSignalsJob reads it → FuturesExecutor places order. Add a VCR-cassette-backed integration spec covering the happy path and the kill-switch/paper-mode guard paths.

title: Add observability: metrics and structured logging for order flow
description: Order placement, signal generation, and position reconciliation emit plain logger.info strings. Add structured logging (e.g. tagged logger or JSON log fields) and optionally emit metrics (StatsD/Prometheus counters) for: signals fired, orders placed, orders rejected, reconciliation diffs, and errors. This feeds dashboards and alerting.

title: Implement FuturesExecutor contract rollover logic
description: FuturesExecutor#perform_rollover has a TODO for the actual rollover logic. The contract detection and target resolution are present but the close-old / open-new order flow is not implemented. Should close positions in expiring contracts and re-open equivalent size on the target month contract.

title: Implement FuturesExecutor basis check via live REST mark price
description: FuturesExecutor#consider_entry has a TODO to query futures best bid/ask or mark price via REST to compute real basis bps. Currently it uses spot_price as a placeholder, so the basis gate never triggers meaningfully. Wire in CoinbaseRest or AdvancedTradeClient to fetch the mark/best-bid-ask for the target futures product.

title: Convert remaining buy/sell signal consumers to long/short at API boundary
description: SignalAlert.create_entry_signal! now normalizes buy→long / sell→short, but other consumers of raw signal side strings (e.g. strategy outputs, executor calls) may still pass buy/sell. Audit all callers and ensure long/short are the canonical values end-to-end so the side normalizer is not needed at runtime.

title: Persist CalibrationJob best params to a settings store
description: CalibrationJob runs a grid search and logs the best tp_target/sl_target per symbol but never persists the results. Add a simple key-value settings store (or a dedicated table/model) and write the calibrated params so trading strategies can load them at runtime instead of using hardcoded defaults.

title: Refactor remaining receive_message_chain specs to use real records
description: Several spec files still use heavy receive_message_chain doubles that make tests brittle and hard to maintain. Continue the pattern established in health_check and realtime specs: use real DB records and real service objects where cheap, replacing chains of mocked relations.

title: Clean up stringio gem ambiguity warning in Ruby environment
description: A repeated stringio gem ambiguity warning appears during RSpec runs (noted in session logs). Identify whether it comes from Gemfile, a transitive dep, or the RVM gemset, and resolve it so test output is clean.
